questions.question: A patient is prescribed $2\sqrt{5}$ milligrams of medication. If the patient already took $\sqrt{5}$ milligrams, how much more medication does the patient need?
The number of cells in a culture grows at a rate of $2\sqrt{t}$ per hour, where $t$ is the time in hours. If the initial number of cells is $\sqrt{2}$, how many cells will be present after $2$ hours?

A medical researcher is analyzing data on the effectiveness of a new treatment. The data shows a correlation of $0.8$ between the treatment and patient outcomes, measured in units of $\sqrt{5}$. If the researcher wants to calculate the correlation in terms of $\sqrt{2}$, what would be the equivalent value?

The probability of a certain medical condition occurring is $\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$. If the probability of the condition occurring twice is $\frac{1}{2}$, what is the probability that it will occur at least once?

In a study on the effects of a new medication, the average response time was $3\sqrt{2}$ minutes. If the standard deviation of the response times was $\sqrt{5}$ minutes, what percentage of patients responded within one standard deviation of the average?

A pharmaceutical company is developing a new drug that has a $2\sqrt{3}$% chance of being effective. If the company wants to calculate the probability that at least $2$ out of $5$ patients will respond to the treatment, what is the probability?

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Make sure to simplify all radical expressions before solving the problem
Tip 2: Pay attention to units when working with word problems
Tip 3: Double-check calculations to ensure accuracy
